text.content:
Settings live in Game Settings > Module Settings > Ionrift Respite. Each setting carries its own hint, so this page sticks to the things the panel cannot show in a hover.

Starting a rest

Only the GM starts a rest. Three ways:

  • Type /respite in chat.
  • Click the campground icon in the token controls on the left of the canvas.
  • Let a player click Long Rest on their character sheet. With Intercept Player Rests on (default), the Respite flow opens instead of the native dialog.

Quick Setup profiles

The top of the settings panel offers Simple, Standard, and Survival presets. Pick the closest one, then tune in the panels below. Standard is the recommended starting point.

Comfort, in detail

Comfort is the headline mechanic. The GM sets the baseline from the environment. Shelter, fire, gear, and certain camp activities shift it up or down. The tier the rest resolves at determines:

TierHP recoveryHit Dice penaltyExhaustion saveEncounter pressure
SafeFullNoneNoneNone (skipped)
ShelteredFullNoneNoneNormal
RoughFull−1 HDCON DC 10 or +1 exhaustionRaised
Hostile75% of max−2 HDCON DC 15 or +1 exhaustion; existing exhaustion cannot clearHigh

The comfort badge anywhere in the UI shows the same hint on hover. Disable comfort entirely with Comfort Rules in the settings panel for a simplified rest.

Terrains and events

Terrain sets the event pool and the baseline encounter DC. Each ships with its own pool: forest, desert, swamp, coast, arctic, underdark, urban, ruins, dungeon, tavern.

Events resolve on a four-tier outcome scale (triumph, success, mixed, failure) shaped by how far the GM's roll lands above or below the night's DC. Categories: Encounter (something hostile), Complication (a problem to solve), Discovery (something useful), Ambient (colour, no mechanics).

Curating the pool

Camp events only fire if you have added them to the active pool. Open Module Settings > Ionrift Respite > Curate Event Pool, or click Curate Pool from the Content Packs panel. Until the pool has entries, a low night-check roll simply passes with no event, so you can read through everything before anything can surprise the table.

Meals and diets

Off by default. Turn it on under Food & Diet if you want rations and water tracked at camp. Per-character dietary rules and preset profiles live in the same dialog.

If a rest gets stuck

Reset Rest State in the settings panel (under Tools) clears any lock and removes camp tokens from the active scene. Use it when a rest cannot start, the party needs to rest again the same in-game day, or a previous rest did not finish cleanly.
